verification.summary: Exit 0; manifest JSON syntax is valid.
Exit 0 with no matches; there were no allowed hits to explain.
Ported ECI/NotebookEdit/session-ownership cases pass; full suite reports SUMMARY pass=117 fail=33 xfail=0 xpass=0 todo=0, with remaining failures in broader stop-gate/reviewer groups.

active_behaviors.name: SessionStart
active_behaviors.summary: hooks.json wires SessionStart matcher startup|resume|clear to hooks/session-snapshot.sh; the script stores a stop-proof baseline when possible, clears legacy skip_stop, prunes old proof-adjacent state, and emits the local guidance reminder.

active_behaviors.name: UserPromptSubmit
active_behaviors.summary: hooks.json wires UserPromptSubmit to hooks/prompt-task-reminder.sh; the script records reviewer prompt HEAD, clears reviewer bypass files, and records /side prompt state.

active_behaviors.name: PreToolUse validation
active_behaviors.summary: Bash runs validate-bash.sh and the opt-in first-tool reviewer. apply_patch, Edit, Write, MultiEdit, and NotebookEdit run file-edit validators, the security reminder, ECI and ATE gates where wired, and the opt-in first-tool reviewer. Edit validators reject protected session-owned paths when the current session is not an owner.

active_behaviors.name: ECI gate
active_behaviors.summary: eci-active-gate.sh blocks main-thread code edits while session-scoped ECI state is active at $CODEX_PROOF_ROOT/$SESSION_ID/eci_active, allows markdown-only edits, and exempts spawned subagents only through transcript metadata. It ignores legacy cwd-scoped ECI markers. CODEX_ROLE is not an ECI bypass.

active_behaviors.name: ATE gate
active_behaviors.summary: ate-orchestrator-gate.sh treats CODEX_ROLE=lead and CODEX_ROLE=coordinator as legacy hook metadata that blocks direct edits. Standard teammates use spawn_agent, send_input, and wait_agent; CODEX_ROLE must not launch teammates.

active_behaviors.name: Stop gate and internal reviewer
active_behaviors.summary: hooks.json wires only hooks/stop-gate.sh for Stop. stop-gate.sh invokes hooks/system-prompt-reviewer.sh internally; that reviewer is opt-in and fail-open, and only runs when CODEX_STOP_REVIEWER parses to a supported backend.

active_behaviors.name: Subagent exemptions
active_behaviors.summary: Stop, ECI, and reviewer exemptions depend on Codex transcript metadata: first transcript event type session_meta with .payload.source.subagent.thread_spawn. They do not depend on agent_id, CODEX_ROLE, or shell-launched wrappers.

active_behaviors.name: skip-stop
active_behaviors.summary: bin/skip-stop manages session-scoped or cwd-scoped skip_stop markers. stop-gate.sh allows a fresh marker younger than 60 minutes, and session-snapshot.sh prunes stale marker state.

active_behaviors.name: eci-active CLI
active_behaviors.summary: bin/eci-active manages only session-scoped ECI markers under $CODEX_PROOF_ROOT/$SESSION_ID/eci_active. Without CODEX_SESSION_ID, it targets the newest session directory under the proof root; it never creates cwd-scoped ECI state. off requires a validated disengage report before removing the marker.

active_behaviors.name: /side stop behavior
active_behaviors.summary: A prompt beginning with /side writes side-stop markers for the parent session and cwd. stop-gate.sh allows a different session covered by that marker, but the parent session remains subject to active ECI blocking.

non_ports.name: skill marker hooks
non_ports.source_behavior: Claude wires PostToolUse matcher Skill to skill-marker-record.sh and PreToolUse matcher Edit|Write|MultiEdit to skill-marker-gate.sh.
non_ports.codex_behavior: Codex has no active PostToolUse Skill marker wiring. Skill routing remains instruction-only through CODEX.md, developer instructions, and skill trigger metadata.
guardrails: Do not add Codex skill marker record wiring until sanitized active Codex payload evidence exposes a skill identity field.
Do not add a Codex skill marker gate until sanitized active Codex payload evidence exposes both skill identity and edit target path fields.

degraded_or_unsupported.name: fail-open reviewers
degraded_or_unsupported.summary: Stop and first-tool reviewers are opt-in and fail open. Missing env vars, malformed backend specs, failed calls, and malformed reviewer verdicts skip review rather than blocking.

degraded_or_unsupported.name: unsupported reviewer backend forms
degraded_or_unsupported.summary: Supported reviewer backend specs are limited to ollama:URL:MODEL and opencode-zen:URL:MODEL. Credential-oriented, shell-oriented, and CODEX_ROLE-oriented backend specs are not supported.

degraded_or_unsupported.name: silent reviewer pass
degraded_or_unsupported.summary: A reviewer verdict of pass exits 0 without producing a positive proof artifact; fail verdicts can block with filtered violations.

degraded_or_unsupported.name: transcript-metadata-dependent subagent detection
degraded_or_unsupported.summary: Subagent exemption depends on transcript_path pointing under $HOME/.codex/sessions and the first event containing .payload.source.subagent.thread_spawn. Without that shape, hooks treat the context as a main session.

degraded_or_unsupported.name: rust analyzer substitute
degraded_or_unsupported.summary: Claude enabled rust-analyzer-lsp. Codex has omx_code_intel enabled, but this is not rust-analyzer parity.

degraded_or_unsupported.name: memory import not claimed
degraded_or_unsupported.summary: Codex has omx_memory enabled in local config. This ledger does not claim Claude memory data was imported because no verified import artifact was found in the inspected local files.
